6.text: Wheat prices are forecast to fall by 0.1% from Rs.2,406 to Rs.2,403/quintal over the next three months (model accuracy: 1.5% error), driven primarily by seasonal harvest cycle, MSP floor support, and structural demand trends. Seasonal dynamics show the market transitioning toward the next harvest window, with supply availability remaining adequate and pricing reflecting the typical inter-seasonal softness. With prices near or below the MSP of Rs.2,600/quintal, government procurement is expected to intensify, providing strong price support and limiting further downside in the coming weeks. The ensemble model's highest-weighted predictors are: **price-to-MSP ratio (floor support)**, **price premium/discount vs. government MSP**, and **30-day price volatility** - together capturing the bearish price signal in historical patterns across 25+ years of mandi data. The 90-day seasonal forecast incorporates annual price cycles, monsoon-linked Kharif sowing sentiment, and the year-on-year MSP increase of Rs.175/quintal (+7.2%) which anchors longer-term price expectations.

6.text: Mustard prices are forecast to rise by 0.5% from Rs.6,999 to Rs.7,033/quintal over the next three months (model accuracy: 1.9% error), driven primarily by seasonal harvest cycle, MSP floor support, and structural demand trends. With 3 months until the next mustard harvest, the market is in a lean-supply inter-seasonal phase where prices typically firm up as old-crop stocks diminish and fresh arrivals are limited. Trading at a 13% premium to the 2026 MSP of Rs.6,200/quintal, prices reflect strong private demand and limited export competition while the MSP provides a comfortable downside buffer. The ensemble model's highest-weighted predictors are: **price-to-MSP ratio (floor support)**, **Vol Log Ret 14**, and **Roll Cv 7** - together capturing the bullish price signal in historical patterns across 25+ years of mandi data. The 90-day seasonal forecast incorporates annual price cycles, monsoon-linked Kharif sowing sentiment, and the year-on-year MSP increase of Rs.250/quintal (+4.2%) which anchors longer-term price expectations.
